full_response:
We want the scalar obtained by first multiplying the row vector v by the matrix [M] and then by the column vector u.  In components:

1. Label the components  
   v = (v₁, v₂, v₃), u = (u₁, u₂, u₃)ᵀ, [M] = (M_{ij}) with i,j = 1,2,3.

2. First multiply v by [M]:  
   (v·M)_j = Σ_{i=1}^{3} v_i M_{ij}.  
   This gives another row vector whose j-th component is the above sum.

3. Now dot this result with u:  
   v·M·u = Σ_{j=1}^{3} (v·M)_j u_j  
          = Σ_{j=1}^{3} [Σ_{i=1}^{3} v_i M_{ij}] u_j.

4. Combine the two summations:  
   v·M·u = Σ_{i=1}^{3} Σ_{j=1}^{3} v_i M_{ij} u_j.

Thus, using Σ-notation,

\boxed{\,v\cdot [M]\cdot u \;=\; \displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{3}\sum_{j=1}^{3} v_i\,M_{ij}\,u_j\,}

Confidence: 93%
